---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0c-parser
key: sv0c-parser
state: draft
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0c
    - parser
    - ast
title: "sv0c parser (recursive descent) and AST definition"
type: task
---

# sv0c parser (recursive descent) and AST definition

## purpose

implement the AST type definitions and recursive descent parser for sv0
in SML/NJ. the parser consumes a token stream (from the lexer) and produces
an untyped AST with full source spans.

the parser must handle:
- all top-level declarations (module, use, fn, struct, enum, trait, impl,
  type, newtype, const, static)
- function signatures with contract clauses
- all expression forms (binary, unary, call, field, index, cast, match,
  if, closures, blocks)
- all statement forms (let, assignment, expression, return)
- pattern matching (exhaustive match arms with guards)
- operator precedence and associativity
- error recovery (panic mode: skip to next statement boundary)

## dependencies

/require: sml

## configuration

/env: SV0C_ROOT=sv0c
/env: SRC_DIR=sv0c/src
/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc

## implementation

### step 1: AST type definitions

/ai: implement the AST datatypes in SML/NJ for sv0c.

     create src/ast/ast.sml with algebraic datatypes representing
     the complete sv0 abstract syntax tree.

     the AST should include:
     - program: list of top-level declarations
     - declarations: Module, Use, FnDecl, StructDecl, EnumDecl,
       TraitDecl, ImplDecl, TypeAlias, Newtype, ConstDecl, StaticDecl
     - FnDecl: name, params, return type, contracts, body, visibility
     - contracts: Requires of expr | Ensures of expr |
       LoopInvariant of expr | Borrows of ident list
     - type expressions: NamedType, ArrayType, SliceType, TupleType,
       RefType, MutRefType, FnType, GenericType
     - expressions: Literal, Ident, Binary, Unary, Call, MethodCall,
       FieldAccess, Index, Cast, If, Match, Block, Closure, ForIn,
       While, WhileLet, IfLet, Return, Try (?), Assign
     - patterns: WildcardPat, BindPat, LitPat, TuplePat, StructPat,
       EnumPat, OrPat, GuardedPat
     - statements: LetStmt, ExprStmt, ReturnStmt
     - literals: IntLit, FloatLit, StringLit, BoolLit, CharLit

     every AST node should carry a Span.span field.
     reference: sv0doc/grammar/sv0.ebnf

/run: 01-define-ast.sh

### step 2: recursive descent parser

/ai: implement the recursive descent parser in SML/NJ for sv0c.

     create src/parser/parser.sml with:

     signature PARSER = sig
       val parse : Lexer.located_token list -> Ast.program
       val parseExpr : Lexer.located_token list ->
                       Ast.expr * Lexer.located_token list
     end

     the parser should:
     - use recursive descent (no parser generator)
     - implement Pratt parsing for expression precedence
     - handle all sv0 operator precedence levels
     - parse contract clauses (requires, ensures, loop_invariant, borrows)
       between function signature and body
     - parse generic type parameters and trait bounds
     - implement error recovery: on syntax error, skip tokens until a
       statement/declaration boundary (semicolon, closing brace, keyword)
       and continue parsing to report multiple errors
     - produce meaningful error messages with source spans

     operator precedence (lowest to highest):
     1. assignment (=)
     2. logical or (||)
     3. logical and (&&)
     4. equality (==, !=)
     5. comparison (<, >, <=, >=)
     6. bitwise or (|)
     7. bitwise xor (^)
     8. bitwise and (&)
     9. shift (<<, >>)
     10. additive (+, -)
     11. multiplicative (*, /, %)
     12. unary (-, !, ~, &, &mut, *)
     13. postfix (., (), [], as, ?)

     reference: sv0doc/grammar/sv0.ebnf

/run: 02-build-parser.sh

### step 3: parser tests

/ai: write a comprehensive test suite for the sv0c parser in SML/NJ.

     create sv0c/test/parser_test.sml with tests for:
     - simple let bindings (let x: i32 = 42;)
     - function declarations with and without contracts
     - struct and enum declarations
     - match expressions with all pattern types
     - operator precedence (verify AST structure)
     - if/else as expressions
     - for..in loops
     - closures (arrow syntax)
     - generic type parameters
     - module and use declarations
     - error recovery (malformed input still produces partial AST)

/run: 03-test-parser.sh

## validation

/assert: test -f $SRC_DIR/ast/ast.sml
/assert: test -f $SRC_DIR/parser/parser.sml
